While checking SCADA I noticed that WWEL_FIT1 was showing a flow of approx 25L/s.  This reading on SCADA is for the overflow to the river.  Based on the SCADA trends this flow started to creep up from 0 L/s on March 7.  The trends did not indicate the wetwell level getting to an elevation that would cause an overflow event.  At 1113HRS I contacted Kevin Kerek to notify him of the findings.  He confirmed that he will dispatch an operator to check out the station and confirm the overflow weir is dry and follow-up as needed with instrumentation.

- Plant water booster pump is cycling too much. This eventually causes pumps to fault on high and low pressure. This issue seems to have been going on for a while.

Upon inspection, it seems RT1 and RT2 temperature protection relay faulted and is showing red. In addition, the discharge side fails to maintain adequate pressure while the plant water demand is low (vortex water scour not active at the time, no plant water usage from hydrant, etc). This is causing the pump to cycle on constantly to boost the pressure.

The pressure tank seems to be okay with no leaks. Pressure switch differential settings seem okay. The check valve seems to be the culprit. I?ve tried to isolate the lead pump, pump 1, and the hissing sound stopped but the system still loses pressure fast. Therefore, it seems to be leaking on both sides. Created work order for maintenance and notified team

Received call from ROC at 08:07 about filter 2 back wash interlocked and OOS.
When signed into SCADA found that both filter 1 and 2 were going to have a backwash
at the same time. Also found that low lift pump 3 was having un-commanded stops,
so put pump 3 to manual off at 08:24. Filter 2 will not be able to do its backwash till the backwash
tank level drops down low enough for it to start. Will inform ROC of what is happening.
Completed filter performance check: filter 1- 100%, filter 2- 100%

11:11 remote Filter performance check. Both filters are at 100% performance. No issues found.

Received call from ROC regarding low CL2 influent. Plant not running.

LDTEXT:
-On site to respond to Clarifier 2 Fault Alarm, reported by ROC.
-Found Clarifier 2 with a Torque Alarm on MCC.
-Manually backed up clarifier 2 arm, cleared faults, and ran in auto.  Repeated this several times, each time Clarifier 2 would run a little bit longer before the Torque Alarm came back in.  Inspected clarifier gearbox and found that heater was running, but gearbox was full of chunks of a frozen water/oil mixture.  Drained as much water as possible from gearbox and added in new oil.  Turned up temperature on heater slightly and used propane torch to help thaw out gearbox.  Torque reading on Clarifier 2 has dropped from approximately 45 to 25 and is continuing to slowly decrease.  Left heater running, but repositioned against opposite wall.
-Clarifier 2 is back in auto and running.
DESCRIPTION: Mount Albert Water Resource Recovery Facility

-On site to respond to communications failure alarm reported by ROC.  PCS on-call has also been notified and is on route to site.
-0405 - Upon arrival to site, swipe card reader not working, local SCADA OIT panel is blank and not working, Pumps both displaying No Communication faults on panels.  Wet well level is at 4.08m.  Tried resetting pump faults on local panel, but pumps will not run in remote or local.  Wet well backup mode is in Auto, but pumps not running.
-Called on-call electrician, tried cycling power to pumps, but still not working.  Tried disconnecting power for 5-10min, but pumps still not working when turned back on.  On-call electrician on route to site.
-0431 - Contacted ROC to initiate emergency vac truck procedure and have trucks dispatched to site.
-Tried transferring site to generator power, suspecting possible phase loss on site.  Unable to get generator to turn on, e-key not working on any locks on site.
-Briefly left site to bump e-key at MTA WRRF.  When back on site, e-key still not working.  Cut lock to generator building.  Generator screen is blank and unresponsive.  Generator will not run in remote or manual.
-0512 - Wessuc confirmed they have vac truck and tanker on route to site.
-Electricians and PCS arrived at site and found multiple damaged pieces of equipment due to a suspected lightning strike on the building.  They immediately began work to restore functionality of damaged equipment.
-0631 - Wessuc informed of delays and that they were still approximately 1.5hrs away.
-0645 - Informed SAC of ongoing spill.  Spoke with environmental officer Edhilvia Campos and was assigned event# 1QG1QRG.
-0655 - Informed York Region local health unit of ongoing spill.  Spoke with health officer David Huang.  
-0700 - Notified LSCRA of ongoing spill.
-0815 - Wessuc arrived on site and began setting up hoses to pump down wet well.  Began pumping down wet well at approximately 0830.
-0830 - Collected spill samples with Chris Rodie, CoC# W11769441.
-0925 - Electricians able to get one pump running on site and began pumping down wet well to forcemain along with Wessuc pumping wet well to vac truck.
-0957 - Wet well at 3.99m, below the overflow level.  Spill has ended.
-Kevin Shaji arrived at site to complete weekly sample collection.
-When Wessuc had completed their emergency wet well pumping activities, accompanied vac truck to site of spill, which was a local East Gwillimbury manhole between the southeast corner of the Foodland parking lot and the railroad tracks.  Wessuc cleaned solids and liquid from surrounding area, as well as removed excess solids from local sewer.  While on site, visited by local Public Heath Inspector Darja Montsenko, who also observed spill location during cleanup and afterwards.
-Instrumentation team on site to replace damaged flowmeter and level sensor.
-Electricians and PCS team able to get both station pumps repaired and operational in auto.
-Temporary generator set up on site and tested in manual and in auto, with a full station transfer test.
-Composite sampler reset for 0700 April 7, 2026.  Sample jug cleaned.
-Security system and swipe card reader on site not working.  Informed supervisor of issue and work request created.
-Station is back running normally.  Temporary generator set up on site.  Both pumps available and in local.
